content:
# Cushion Gas

**Summary**: A base gas used in underground storage to maintain pressure and provide a safety buffer.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024]] evaluated the impact of different cushion gases (CH4, N2, CO2) on hydrogen storage performance.

## Scientific Context

- [[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024]] Crucial for determining the viability of depleted reservoirs as hydrogen storage sites.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024]] Comparison of different gases (CH4, N2, CO2) across various formulations.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- CH4 was found to be the best for maintaining high gas columns at various conditions. ([[2024-muhammed|Muhammed, 2024, p. 34]])
